heading: Advance medical directives of members and dependents: requirement for recognition by States
label: 1044c Advance medical directives of members and dependents: requirement for recognition by States
notes: Effective Date of 1996 Amendment, , , provided that:Pub. L. 104–106, div. A, title VII, § 749(b)110 Stat. 389Feb. 10, 1996“, United States Code, shall take effect on the date of the enactment of this Act [] and shall apply to advance medical directives referred to in that section that are executed before, on, or after that date.”Section 1044c of title 10Feb. 10, 1996

children.heading: .—Instruments To Be Given Legal Effect Without Regard to State Law
children.label: a .—Instruments To Be Given Legal Effect Without Regard to State Law

children.text: is exempt from any requirement of form, substance, formality, or recording that is provided for advance medical directives under the laws of a State; and
shall be given the same legal effect as an advance medical directive prepared and executed in accordance with the laws of the State concerned.

sets forth directions regarding the provision, withdrawal, or withholding of life-prolonging procedures, including hydration and sustenance, for the declarant whenever the declarant has a terminal physical condition or is in a persistent vegetative state; or
authorizes another person to make health care decisions for the declarant, under circumstances stated in the declaration, whenever the declarant is incapable of making informed health care decisions.

Under regulations prescribed by the Secretary concerned, an advance medical directive prepared by an attorney authorized to provide legal assistance shall contain a statement that sets forth the provisions of subsection (a).
Paragraph (1) shall not be construed to make inapplicable the provisions of subsection (a) to an advance medical directive that does not include a statement described in that paragraph.

children.heading: .—States Not Recognizing Advance Medical Directives
children.label: d .—States Not Recognizing Advance Medical Directives
children.text: Subsection (a) does not make an advance medical directive enforceable in a State that does not otherwise recognize and enforce advance medical directives under the laws of the State.
The term “State” includes the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, and a possession of the United States.
The term “person eligible for legal assistance” means a person who is eligible for legal assistance under .section 1044 of this title
The term “legal assistance” means legal services authorized under .section 1044 of this title
